Assessment of Bank Secrecy Act No-Action Letters, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–283, div. F, title LXIII, § 6305134 Stat. 4587Jan. 1, 2021“(a)Assessment.—“(1).—In generalThe Director [probably means Director of FinCEN], in consultation with the Attorney General, the Federal functional regulators, State bank supervisors, State credit union supervisors, and other Federal agencies, as appropriate, shall conduct an assessment on whether to establish a process for the issuance of no-action letters by FinCEN [Financial Crimes Enforcement Network of the Department of the Treasury] in response to inquiries from persons concerning the application of the Bank Secrecy Act, the USA PATRIOT Act (; ), section 8(s) of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (), or any other anti-money laundering or countering the financing of terrorism law (including regulations) to specific conduct, including a request for a statement as to whether FinCEN or any relevant Federal functional regulator intends to take an enforcement action against the person with respect to such conduct.Public Law 107–56115 Stat. 27212 U.S.C. 1818(s)“(2).—AnalysisThe assessment required under paragraph (1) shall include an analysis of—“(A)a timeline for the process used to reach a final determination by FinCEN, in consultation with the relevant Federal functional regulators, in response to a request by a person for a no-action letter;“(B)whether improvements in current processes are necessary;“(C)whether a formal no-action letter process would help to mitigate or accentuate illicit finance risks in the United States; and“(D)any other matter the Secretary determines is appropriate.“(b).—Report and RulemakingsNot later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Secretary [of the Treasury], in coordination with the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation, the Attorney General, the Secretary of Homeland Security, and the Federal functional regulators, shall—Jan. 1, 2021“(1)submit to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives a report that contains all findings and determinations made in carrying out the assessment required under subsection (a); and“(2)propose rulemakings, if appropriate, to implement the findings and determinations described in paragraph (1).”[For definition of “Federal functional regulator” as used in , set out above, see , set out as a Definitions note under .]section 6305 of Pub. L. 116–283section 6003 of Pub. L. 116–283section 5311 of this title

Establishment of Highly Secure Network, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 107–56, title III, § 362115 Stat. 332Pub. L. 108–458, title VI, § 6202(m)118 Stat. 3746Oct. 26, 2001Dec. 17, 2004“(a).—In GeneralThe Secretary [of the Treasury] shall establish a highly secure network in the Financial Crimes Enforcement Network that—“(1)allows financial institutions to file reports required under subchapter II of chapter 53 of title 31, United States Code, chapter 2 of [probably means chapter 2 (§§ 121 to 129) of title I of ( et seq.)], or section 21 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act [] through the secure network; andPublic Law 91–508Pub. L. 91–50812 U.S.C. 195112 U.S.C. 1829b“(2)provides financial institutions with alerts and other information regarding suspicious activities that warrant immediate and enhanced scrutiny.“(b).—Expedited DevelopmentThe Secretary shall take such action as may be necessary to ensure that the secure network required under subsection (a) is fully operational before the end of the 9-month period beginning on the date of enactment of this Act [].”Oct. 26, 2001

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The Financial Crimes Enforcement Network established by order of the Secretary of the Treasury (Treasury Order Numbered 105–08, in this section referred to as “FinCEN”) on , shall be a bureau in the Department of the Treasury.April 25, 1990

children.heading: .—Appointment
children.label: 1 .—Appointment
children.text: The head of FinCEN shall be the Director, who shall be appointed by the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.label: A
children.text: Advise and make recommendations on matters relating to financial intelligence, financial criminal activities, and other financial activities to the Under Secretary of the Treasury for Enforcement.

children.label: i
children.text: Information collected by the Department of the Treasury, including report information filed under subchapter II of chapter 53 of this title (such as reports on cash transactions, foreign financial agency transactions and relationships, foreign currency transactions, exporting and importing monetary instruments, and suspicious activities), chapter 2 of title I of , and section 21 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act.Public Law 91–508

children.label: ii
children.text: Information regarding national and international currency flows.

children.label: iii
children.text: Other records and data maintained by other Federal, State, local, and foreign agencies, including financial and other records developed in specific cases.

children.label: iv
children.text: Other privately and publicly available information.

children.label: i
children.text: identify possible criminal activity to appropriate Federal, State, local, Tribal, and foreign law enforcement agencies;

children.label: ii
children.text: support ongoing criminal financial investigations and prosecutions and related proceedings, including civil and criminal tax and forfeiture proceedings;

children.label: iii
children.text: identify possible instances of noncompliance with subchapter II of chapter 53 of this title, chapter 2 of title I of , and section 21 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to Federal agencies with statutory responsibility for enforcing compliance with such provisions and other appropriate Federal regulatory agencies;Public Law 91–508

children.label: iv
children.text: evaluate and recommend possible uses of special currency reporting requirements under section 5326;

children.label: v
children.text: determine emerging trends and methods in money laundering and other financial crimes;

children.label: vi
children.text: support the conduct of intelligence or counterintelligence activities, including analysis, to protect against terrorism; and

children.label: vii
children.text: support government initiatives against money laundering.

children.label: D
children.text: Establish and maintain a financial crimes communications center to furnish law enforcement authorities with intelligence information related to emerging or ongoing investigations and undercover operations.

children.label: E
children.text: Furnish research, analytical, and informational services to financial institutions, appropriate Federal regulatory agencies with regard to financial institutions, and appropriate Federal, State, local, Tribal, and foreign law enforcement authorities, in accordance with policies and guidelines established by the Secretary of the Treasury or the Under Secretary of the Treasury for Enforcement, in the interest of detection, prevention, and prosecution of terrorism, organized crime, money laundering, and other financial crimes.

children.label: F
children.text: Assist Federal, State, local, Tribal, and foreign law enforcement and regulatory authorities in combatting the use of informal, nonbank networks and payment and barter system mechanisms that permit the transfer of funds or the equivalent of funds without records and without compliance with criminal and tax laws.

children.label: G
children.text: Provide computer and data support and data analysis to the Secretary of the Treasury for tracking and controlling foreign assets.

children.label: H
children.text: Coordinate with financial intelligence units in other countries on anti-terrorism and anti-money laundering initiatives, and similar efforts.

children.label: I
children.text: Administer the requirements of subchapter II of chapter 53 of this title, chapter 2 of title I of , and section 21 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act, to the extent delegated such authority by the Secretary of the Treasury.Public Law 91–508

children.label: J
children.text: Promulgate regulations under section 5318(h)(4)(D), as appropriate, to implement the government-wide anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism priorities established by the Secretary of the Treasury under section 5318(h)(4)(A).

children.label: K
children.text: Communicate regularly with financial institutions and Federal functional regulators that examine financial institutions for compliance with subchapter II of chapter 53 and regulations promulgated under that subchapter and law enforcement authorities to explain the United States Government’s anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism priorities.

children.label: L
children.text: Give and receive feedback to and from financial institutions, State bank supervisors, and State credit union supervisors (as those terms are defined in section 6003 of the Anti-Money Laundering Act of 2020) regarding the matters addressed in subchapter II of chapter 53 and regulations promulgated under that subchapter.

children.label: M
children.text: Maintain money laundering and terrorist financing investigation financial experts capable of identifying, tracking, and analyzing financial crime networks and identifying emerging threats to support Federal civil and criminal investigations.

children.label: N
children.text: Maintain emerging technology experts to encourage the development of and identify emerging technologies that can assist the United States Government or financial institutions in countering money laundering and the financing of terrorism.

children.label: O
children.text: Such other duties and powers as the Secretary of the Treasury may delegate or prescribe.

children.heading: .—Requirements Relating to Maintenance and Use of Data Banks
children.label: c .—Requirements Relating to Maintenance and Use of Data Banks
A
children.text: the submission of reports through the Internet or other secure network, whenever possible;

children.label: B
children.text: the cataloguing of information in a manner that facilitates rapid retrieval by law enforcement personnel of meaningful data; and

children.label: C
children.text: a procedure that provides for a prompt initial review of suspicious activity reports and other reports, or such other means as the Secretary may provide, to identify information that warrants immediate action; and

children.label: A
children.text: who is to be given access to the information maintained by FinCEN;

children.label: B
children.text: what limits are to be imposed on the use of such information; and

children.label: C
children.text: how information about activities or relationships which involve or are closely associated with the exercise of constitutional rights is to be screened out of the data maintenance system.

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: 1 .—Establishment
children.text: The FinCEN Exchange is hereby established within FinCEN.

children.label: i
children.text: under subchapter II of chapter 53 and the regulations promulgated under that subchapter; and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to other anti-money laundering requirements;

children.label: B
children.text: protect the financial system from illicit use; and

children.label: C
children.text: promote national security.

children.label: I
children.text: the results of those efforts; and

children.label: II
children.text: the extent and effectiveness of those efforts, including any benefits realized by law enforcement agencies from partnering with financial institutions and other relevant private sector entities, which shall be consistent with standards protecting sensitive information; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any legislative, administrative, or other recommendations the Secretary may have to strengthen the efforts of the FinCEN Exchange.

children.heading: .—Classified annex
children.label: B .—Classified annex
children.text: Each report under subparagraph (A) may include a classified annex.

children.label: A
children.text: in compliance with all other applicable Federal laws and regulations;

children.label: B
children.text: in such a manner as to ensure the appropriate confidentiality of personal information; and

children.label: C
children.text: at the discretion of the Director, with the appropriate Federal functional regulator, as defined in section 6003 of the Anti-Money Laundering Act of 2020.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: A .—Regulations
children.text: FinCEN shall, as appropriate, promulgate regulations that establish procedures for the protection of information shared and exchanged between FinCEN and the private sector in accordance with this section, consistent with the capacity, size, and nature of the financial institution or other relevant private sector entity to which the particular procedures apply.

children.heading: .—Use by financial institutions
children.label: i .—Use by financial institutions
children.text: Information received by a financial institution pursuant to this section shall not be used for any purpose other than identifying and reporting on activities that may involve the financing of terrorism, money laundering, proliferation financing, or other financial crimes.

children.heading: .—Use by other relevant private sector entities
children.label: ii .—Use by other relevant private sector entities
children.text: Information received by a relevant private sector entity that is not a financial institution pursuant to this section shall not be used for any purpose other than assisting a financial institution in identifying and reporting on activities that may involve the financing of terrorism, money laundering, proliferation financing, or other financial crimes, or in assisting FinCEN or another agency of the Federal Government in mitigating the risk of the financing of terrorism, money laundering, proliferation financing, or other criminal activities.

children.heading: .—Rule of construction
children.label: 6 .—Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection may be construed to create new information sharing authorities or requirements relating to the Bank Secrecy Act.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury may appoint, without regard to the provisions of sections 3309 through 3318 of title 5, candidates directly to positions in the competitive service, as defined in section 2102 of that title, in FinCEN.

children.heading: .—Primary responsibilities
children.label: 2 .—Primary responsibilities
children.text: The primary responsibility of candidates appointed under paragraph (1) shall be to provide substantive support in support of the duties described in subparagraphs (A) through (O) of subsection (b)(2).

children.heading: .—Establishment of office
children.label: 1 .—Establishment of office
children.text: There is established in FinCEN an Office of Domestic Liaison, which shall be headed by the Chief Domestic Liaison.

children.heading: .—Location
children.label: 2 .—Location
children.text: The Office of the Domestic Liaison shall be located in the District of Columbia.

children.label: A
children.text: report directly to the Director; and

children.label: B
children.text: be appointed by the Director, from among individuals with experience or familiarity with anti-money laundering program examinations, supervision, and enforcement.

children.heading: .—Compensation
children.label: 2 .—Compensation
children.text: The annual rate of pay for the Chief Domestic Liaison shall be equal to the highest rate of annual pay for similarly situated senior executives who report to the Director.

children.heading: .—Staff of office
children.label: 3 .—Staff of office
children.text: The Chief Domestic Liaison, with the concurrence of the Director, may retain or employ counsel, research staff, and service staff, as the Liaison determines necessary to carry out the functions, powers, and duties under this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: report to the Chief Domestic Liaison;

children.label: B
children.text: each be assigned to focus on a specific region of the United States; and

children.label: C
children.text: be located at an office in such region or co-located at an office of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System in such region.

children.label: i
children.text: in coordination with relevant Federal functional regulators, perform outreach to BSA officers at financial institutions, including nonbank financial institutions, and persons that are not financial institutions, especially with respect to actions taken by FinCEN that require specific actions by, or have specific effects on, such institutions or persons, as determined by the Director;

children.label: ii
children.text: in accordance with applicable agreements, receive feedback from financial institutions and examiners of Federal functional regulators regarding their examinations under the Bank Secrecy Act and communicate that feedback to FinCEN, the Federal functional regulators, and State bank supervisors;

children.label: iii
children.text: promote coordination and consistency of supervisory guidance from FinCEN, the Federal functional regulators, State bank supervisors, and State credit union supervisors regarding the Bank Secrecy Act;

children.label: iv
children.text: act as a liaison between financial institutions and their Federal functional regulators, State bank supervisors, and State credit union supervisors with respect to information sharing matters involving the Bank Secrecy Act and regulations promulgated thereunder;

children.label: v
children.text: establish safeguards to maintain the confidentiality of communications between the persons described in clause (ii) and the Office of Domestic Liaison;

children.label: vi
children.text: to the extent practicable, periodically propose to the Director changes in the regulations, guidance, or orders of FinCEN, including any legislative or administrative changes that may be appropriate to ensure improved coordination and expand information sharing under this paragraph; and

children.label: vii
children.text: perform such other duties as the Director determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: .—Rule of construction
children.label: B .—Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph may be construed to permit the Domestic Liaisons to have authority over supervision, examination, or enforcement processes.

children.heading: .—Access to documents
children.label: 6 .—Access to documents
children.text: FinCEN, to the extent practicable and consistent with appropriate safeguards for sensitive enforcement-related, pre-decisional, or deliberative information, shall ensure that the Domestic Liaisons have full access to the documents of FinCEN, as necessary to carry out the functions of the Office of Domestic Liaison.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this subsection and every 2 years thereafter for 5 years, the Director shall submit to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives a report on the objectives of the Office of Domestic Liaison for the following fiscal year and the activities of the Office during the immediately preceding fiscal year.

children.label: i
children.text: appropriate statistical information and full and substantive analysis;

children.label: ii
children.text: information on steps that the Office of Domestic Liaison has taken during the reporting period to address feedback received by financial institutions and examiners of Federal functional regulators relating to examinations under the Bank Secrecy Act;

children.label: iii
children.text: recommendations to the Director for such administrative and legislative actions as may be appropriate to address information sharing and coordination issues encountered by financial institutions or examiners of Federal functional regulators; and

children.label: iv
children.text: any other information, as determined appropriate by the Director.

children.heading: .—Sensitive information
children.label: C .—Sensitive information
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (D), FinCEN shall review each report required under subparagraph (A) before the report is submitted to ensure the report does not disclose sensitive information.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Each report required under subparagraph (A) shall be provided directly to the committees listed in that subparagraph, except that a relevant Federal functional regulator, State bank supervisor, Office of Management and Budget, or State credit union supervisor shall have an opportunity for review and comment before the submission of the report.

children.label: I
children.text: sensitive information obtained by a law enforcement agency; and

children.label: II
children.text: classified information.

children.heading: .—Classified information
children.label: E .—Classified information
children.text: No report required under subparagraph (A) may contain classified information.

children.heading: .—Definition
children.label: 8 .—Definition
children.text: In this subsection, the term “Federal functional regulator” has the meaning given the term in section 6003 of the Anti-Money Laundering Act of 2020.

children.heading: FinCEN Foreign Financial Intelligence Unit Liaisons.—
children.label: h FinCEN Foreign Financial Intelligence Unit Liaisons.—
A
children.text: be knowledgeable about domestic or international anti-money laundering or countering the financing of terrorism laws and regulations;

children.label: B
children.text: possess a technical understanding of the Bank Secrecy Act, the protocols of the Egmont Group of Financial Intelligence Units, and the Financial Action Task Force and the recommendations issued by that Task Force;

children.label: C
children.text: be co-located in a United States embassy, a similar United States Government facility, or a foreign government facility, as appropriate;

children.label: D
children.text: facilitate capacity building and perform outreach with respect to anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism regulatory and analytical frameworks;

children.label: E
children.text: establish and maintain relationships with officials from foreign intelligence units, regulatory authorities, ministries of finance, central banks, law enforcement agencies, and other competent authorities;

children.label: F
children.text: participate in industry outreach engagements with foreign financial institutions and other commercial actors on anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism issues;

children.label: G
children.text: coordinate with representatives of the Department of Justice at United States Embassies who perform similar functions on behalf of the United States Government; and

children.label: H
children.text: perform such other duties as the Director determines to be appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: the rate of compensation paid to a Foreign Service officer at a comparable career level serving at the same embassy or facility, as applicable; or

children.label: B
children.text: the rate of compensation that the Liaison would have otherwise received.

children.heading: Protection of Information Obtained by Foreign Law Enforcement and Financial Intelligence Units; Freedom of Information Act.—
children.label: i Protection of Information Obtained by Foreign Law Enforcement and Financial Intelligence Units; Freedom of Information Act.—

children.heading: .—Foreign anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism authority
children.label: A .—Foreign anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism authority
children.text: The term “foreign anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism authority” means any foreign agency or authority that is empowered under foreign law to regulate or supervise foreign financial institutions (or designated non-financial businesses and professions) with respect to laws concerning anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism and proliferation.

children.label: i
children.text: receipt and analysis of suspicious transaction reports and other information relevant to money laundering, associated predicate offenses, and the financing of terrorism; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the dissemination of the results of the analysis described in clause (i).

children.heading: .—Foreign law enforcement authority
children.label: C .—Foreign law enforcement authority
children.text: The term “foreign law enforcement authority” means any foreign agency or authority that is empowered under foreign law to detect, investigate, or prosecute potential violations of law.

children.heading: Information exchanged with foreign law enforcement authorities, foreign financial intelligence units, and foreign anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism authorities.—
children.label: 2 Information exchanged with foreign law enforcement authorities, foreign financial intelligence units, and foreign anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism authorities.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Department of the Treasury may not be compelled to search for or disclose information exchanged with a foreign law enforcement authority, foreign financial intelligence unit, or foreign anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism authority.

children.heading: Inapplicability of freedom of information act.—
children.label: B Inapplicability of freedom of information act.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: (commonly known as the “Freedom of Information Act”) shall not apply to any request for records or information exchanged between the Department of the Treasury and a foreign law enforcement authority, foreign financial intelligence unit, or foreign anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism authority.Section 552(a)(3) of title 5

children.heading: .—Specifically exempted by statute
children.label: ii .—Specifically exempted by statute
children.text: For purposes of , this paragraph shall be considered a statute described in subsection (b)(3)(B) of that section.section 552 of title 5

children.heading: Clarification on information limitations and protections.—
children.label: C Clarification on information limitations and protections.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: The provisions of this paragraph shall apply only to information necessary to exercise the duties and powers described under subsection (b).

children.heading: .—Appropriate confidentiality, classification, and data security requirements
children.label: ii .—Appropriate confidentiality, classification, and data security requirements
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with the Director, shall ensure that information provided to a foreign law enforcement authority, foreign financial intelligence unit, or foreign anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism authority, is subject to appropriate confidentiality, classification, and data security requirements.

children.heading: .—Savings provision
children.label: 3 .—Savings provision
children.text: Nothing in this section shall authorize the Department of the Treasury to withhold information from Congress, decline to carry out a search for information requested by Congress, or prevent the Department of the Treasury from complying with an order of a court of the United States in an action commenced by the United States.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: FinCEN shall maintain financial experts capable of identifying, tracking, and tracing money laundering and terrorist-financing networks in order to conduct and support civil and criminal anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism investigations conducted by the United States Government.

children.heading: .—FinCEN analytical hub
children.label: 2 .—FinCEN analytical hub
children.text: FinCEN, upon a reasonable request from a Federal agency, shall, in collaboration with the requesting agency and the appropriate Federal functional regulator, analyze the potential anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism activity that prompted the request.

children.heading: .—Bank secrecy act
children.label: 1 .—Bank secrecy act
children.text: The term “Bank Secrecy Act” has the meaning given the term in section 6003 of the Anti-Money Laundering Act of 2020.

children.heading: .—Federal functional regulator
children.label: 2 .—Federal functional regulator
children.text: The term “Federal functional regulator” has the meaning given the term in section 509 of the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act ().15 U.S.C. 6809

children.heading: .—Financial institution
children.label: 3 .—Financial institution
children.text: The term “financial institution” has the meaning given the term in .section 5312 of this title

children.heading: .—State bank supervisor
children.label: 4 .—State bank supervisor
children.text: The term “State bank supervisor” has the meaning given the term in section 3 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act ().12 U.S.C. 1813

children.heading: .—State credit union supervisor
children.label: 5 .—State credit union supervisor
children.text: The term “State credit union supervisor” means a State official described in section 107A(e) of the Federal Credit Union Act ().12 U.S.C. 1757a(e)

children.label: A
children.text: $136,000,000 for fiscal year 2021;

children.label: B
children.text: $60,000,000 for fiscal year 2022; and

children.label: C
children.text: $35,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2026.

children.heading: .—Authorization for funding key technological improvements in mission-critical fincen systems
children.label: 2 .—Authorization for funding key technological improvements in mission-critical fincen systems

children.heading: BSA .—direct
children.label: A BSA .—direct
children.text: For technological improvements to provide authorized law enforcement and financial regulatory agencies with Web-based access to FinCEN data, to fully develop and implement the highly secure network required under to expedite the filing of, and reduce the filing costs for, financial institution reports, including suspicious activity reports, collected by FinCEN under chapter 53 and related provisions of law, and enable FinCEN to immediately alert financial institutions about suspicious activities that warrant immediate and enhanced scrutiny, and to provide and upgrade advanced information-sharing technologies to materially improve the Government’s ability to exploit the information in the FinCEN data banks, $16,500,000.section 362 of Public Law 107–56

children.heading: .—Advanced analytical technologies
children.label: B .—Advanced analytical technologies
children.text: To provide advanced analytical tools needed to ensure that the data collected by FinCEN under chapter 53 and related provisions of law are utilized fully and appropriately in safeguarding financial institutions and supporting the war on terrorism, $5,000,000.

children.heading: .—Data networking modernization
children.label: C .—Data networking modernization
children.text: To improve the telecommunications infrastructure to support the improved capabilities of the FinCEN systems, $3,000,000.

children.heading: .—Enhanced compliance capability
children.label: D .—Enhanced compliance capability
children.text: To improve the effectiveness of the Office of Compliance in FinCEN, $3,000,000.

children.heading: .—Detection and prevention of financial crimes and terrorism
children.label: E .—Detection and prevention of financial crimes and terrorism
children.text: To provide development of, and training in the use of, technology to detect and prevent financial crimes and terrorism within and without the United States, $8,000,000.
